behaviors.description: The keyword-frequency analyzer assigns the correct sentiment when the review contains only words from the configured positive and negative word lists.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is local scoring logic (token counting + threshold/rule application). No OS/network/subprocess, no model-output dependence, and no multi-step agent pipeline. REJECTION-GATE: Integration/System/Agentic are disqualified because behavior text is confined to the keyword-frequency analyzer’s own classification rule.
behaviors.test_description: Call the analyzer function/object directly with fixed review text and in-memory positive/negative lists; assert expected sentiment labels across representative cases (all-positive, all-negative, neutral boundary).

behaviors.description: The explanation analyzer produces reasoning that references specific phrases from the original review, not generic boilerplate.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on generated language quality/content (phrase-grounded reasoning vs boilerplate). A stubbed/mocked analyzer would hide the real failure because it can be forced to include phrases. No evidence of multiple agentic stages; this is one analyzer decision. REJECTION-GATE: Workflow is disqualified by behavior text describing a single explanation generation step.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real explanation analyzer on real review inputs; assert output includes concrete phrase overlap/citations from the source review and fails generic-template responses.

behaviors.description: When a review contains mixed signals (both positive and negative keywords), the sentiment score reflects the configured tie-breaking rule.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic rule logic inside the keyword analyzer (mixed counts resolved by configured tie-break). Real infra is unnecessary, and no AI/ML output quality is involved. REJECTION-GATE: Integration is disqualified because behavior text targets rule application, not cross-module handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke analyzer directly with in-memory config specifying tie-break behavior and mixed-keyword reviews; assert the returned sentiment matches each configured tie-break option.

behaviors.description: The explanation for a sarcastic review (e.g., "Great, another broken feature") correctly identifies the negative intent despite positive surface words.
behaviors.justification: This is model judgment about sarcasm/intent, i.e., AI output quality. Deterministic fixtures cannot validate whether the analyzer truly interprets sarcasm. No multi-step agent chain is required by the behavior text. REJECTION-GATE: Workflow is disqualified because only one model decision (explanation interpretation) is specified.
behaviors.test_description: Use the real explanation analyzer on sarcasm-heavy reviews and evaluate whether outputs correctly attribute negative intent despite positive lexical cues.

behaviors.description: Both outputs (score and explanation) are returned together in a single response, with the explanation referencing the same sentiment direction as the score.
behaviors.justification: This includes orchestration plus semantic consistency between deterministic score and generated explanation direction. Mocking explanation generation can hide real mismatches in model-produced direction, so real model output is required. MULTI-STEP does not require Workflow here because only one agentic decision is needed (the explanation output) alongside deterministic scoring. REJECTION-GATE: Workflow is disqualified by lack of multiple sequential model decisions in the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the full service path with real explanation generation and real scoring; assert one response contains both fields and that explanation sentiment direction matches returned score direction.
